eval_instructions: When running these evals, the agent MUST save a workflow-log.md file alongside the generated code. The log must document every phase attempted (Phase 0 through Phase 8), including: agents invoked, commands run (or attempted), questions asked, files read, decisions made, and infrastructure barriers encountered. This is required for grading workflow-level assertions.

evals.prompt: Build a page showing Account records as a gallery of cards. Include name, website, email, phone number. Make the gallery scrollable and each card clickable to open the Account record.
evals.expected_output: The orchestrator creates a working directory, invokes the genpage-planner agent which validates prerequisites (node, pac), authenticates with PAC CLI, runs pac model list-languages, gathers requirements (acknowledges the description, confirms Dataverse entity usage with Account table), detects the account entity exists, checks for available apps and asks user to select one, presents a plan via plan mode, and writes genpage-plan.md. The orchestrator then generates RuntimeTypes.ts via pac model genpage generate-types, invokes genpage-page-builder which reads genpage-plan.md, RuntimeTypes.ts, genpage-rules-reference.md and a relevant sample (8-responsive-cards.tsx), and generates a complete .tsx file using only verified column names. The orchestrator deploys with pac model genpage upload, offers browser verification, and provides a final summary.
expectations: A workflow-log.md file is saved documenting all phases attempted
Phase 0: A working directory is created with a kebab-case name derived from the user's description
Phase 1 (Planner): node --version is run to verify Node.js is installed
Phase 1 (Planner): pac help is run and PAC CLI version >= 2.7.0 is verified
Phase 1 (Planner): Commands are run separately — not chained with &&
Phase 1 (Planner): pac auth list is run to check authentication
Phase 1 (Planner): The active environment is identified and reported to the user
Phase 1 (Planner): pac model list-languages is run after confirming the active environment
Phase 1 (Planner): Question 1 (new or edit) is asked via AskUserQuestion
Phase 1 (Planner): Question 2 (describe the page) is skipped because the user provided a description with the command
Phase 1 (Planner): Question 3 (Dataverse or mock data) is skipped because the description specifies Account table
Phase 1 (Planner): Question 4 (specific requirements) is asked via AskUserQuestion
Phase 1 (Planner): Entity detection runs pac model list-tables --search to check entity existence
Phase 1 (Planner): pac model list is run to check available apps
Phase 1 (Planner): User is asked which app to use (if multiple) or to confirm (if one)
Phase 1 (Planner): A plan is presented via plan mode listing data source (Account entity), features, Fluent UI components, and layout approach
Phase 1 (Planner): User confirmation is requested before proceeding
Phase 1 (Planner): genpage-plan.md is written to the working directory
Phase 2: Entity builder is SKIPPED because account entity already exists
Phase 4: pac model genpage generate-types --data-sources 'account' is run to generate RuntimeTypes.ts in the working directory
Phase 4: RuntimeTypes.ts is read to verify it generated correctly
Phase 5 (Page Builder): genpage-plan.md is read to extract page specification
Phase 5 (Page Builder): RuntimeTypes.ts is read to identify actual column names on the account entity
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Only column names found in RuntimeTypes.ts are used in the generated code — no guessed names
Phase 5 (Page Builder): genpage-rules-reference.md is read before generating code
Phase 5 (Page Builder): A relevant sample file is read (e.g., 8-responsive-cards.tsx for a card layout)
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Generated code uses React 17 + TypeScript with Fluent UI V9 components
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Generated code is a single .tsx file with export default GeneratedComponent
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Generated code uses makeStyles with tokens — not inline styles for static values
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Generated code wraps dataApi calls in try-catch
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Generated code uses @OData.Community.Display.V1.FormattedValue for lookup display names
Phase 5 (Page Builder): No placeholders, TODOs, or ellipses in the generated code
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Code is saved to a .tsx file in the working directory
Phase 6: pac model genpage upload is run with --app-id, --code-file, --data-sources, --prompt, --model, --name, --agent-message, --add-to-sitemap flags
Phase 7: User is asked about browser verification via AskUserQuestion
Phase 8: Final summary includes confirmation, how to find the page, and offer to make updates

evals.prompt: Create a dashboard page with mock data showing sales metrics — monthly revenue chart, top 5 customers table, and a KPI summary bar. Use a modern dark theme look.
evals.expected_output: The orchestrator creates a working directory, invokes genpage-planner which validates prerequisites, authenticates, detects languages, gathers requirements (confirms mock data usage, notes dark theme preference), presents a plan, and writes genpage-plan.md. Entity builder and RuntimeTypes generation are skipped (mock data). The orchestrator invokes genpage-page-builder which reads the plan and rules, generates a complete .tsx with D3.js for the chart, realistic mock data, Fluent UI V9 DataGrid for the table, and styled KPI cards using makeStyles with tokens. Deploys without --data-sources flag.
expectations: A workflow-log.md file is saved documenting all phases attempted
Phase 0: A working directory is created with a kebab-case name
Phase 1 (Planner): node --version and pac help are run separately
Phase 1 (Planner): pac auth list is run and environment is confirmed
Phase 1 (Planner): pac model list-languages is run after confirming the environment
Phase 1 (Planner): Question 1 (new or edit) is asked
Phase 1 (Planner): Question 2 is skipped because user provided a description
Phase 1 (Planner): Question 3 (Dataverse or mock data) is skipped because user said 'mock data'
Phase 1 (Planner): Question 4 (specific requirements) is asked — dark theme is acknowledged
Phase 1 (Planner): Plan mentions mock data, D3.js for chart, DataGrid for customers, KPI cards
Phase 1 (Planner): genpage-plan.md is written to the working directory
Phase 2: Entity builder is SKIPPED (mock data — no entities to create)
Phase 4: RuntimeTypes generation is SKIPPED because mock data was chosen
Phase 5 (Page Builder): genpage-plan.md is read
Phase 5 (Page Builder): genpage-rules-reference.md is read
Phase 5 (Page Builder): A relevant sample is read for reference
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Generated code includes realistic mock data (not empty arrays or placeholder values)
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Chart uses D3.js — no other charting libraries
Phase 5 (Page Builder): D3 code uses group() not nest()
Phase 5 (Page Builder): DataGrid uses createTableColumn from Fluent UI V9
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Code does NOT use 100vh or 100vw
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Code does NOT import from React Router or external libraries beyond React, Fluent UI V9, Fluent icons, D3
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Code does NOT use createTheme, mergeThemes, or useTheme
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Code does NOT wrap content in FluentProvider
Phase 5 (Page Builder): DatePicker (if used) is imported from @fluentui/react-datepicker-compat, not @fluentui/react-components
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Icons use unsized variants (e.g., AddRegular not Add24Regular)
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Generated code is a single .tsx file with export default GeneratedComponent
Phase 6: Deployment command omits --data-sources flag (mock data page)

evals.prompt: I need to edit an existing generative page in my Sales Hub app. The page shows contacts but I want to add a search bar and sorting by company name.
evals.expected_output: The orchestrator invokes genpage-planner which asks Question 1 and the user indicates 'edit existing'. The planner returns { action: 'edit' } and the orchestrator follows the Edit Flow. The orchestrator asks for app-id and page-id, downloads the existing page (producing page.tsx, page.js, config.json, prompt.txt inside a <page-id>/ subfolder), generates RuntimeTypes.ts from config.json.dataSources if needed, invokes genpage-edit-planner to read the download artifacts and produce an edit plan, then applies the plan's changes inline via Edit tool, and deploys with --page-id (no --add-to-sitemap).
expectations: A workflow-log.md file is saved documenting all phases attempted
Phase 1 (Planner): Question 1 is asked and the user answers 'edit existing'
Phase 1 (Planner): Planner returns { action: 'edit' } so Phases 2-8 of the create flow are skipped
Edit Phase 1: User is asked for app-id and page-id via AskUserQuestion
Edit Phase 2: pac model genpage download is run, producing <working-dir>/<page-id>/page.tsx, page.js, config.json, prompt.txt
Edit Phase 3: config.json is read; since dataSources is non-empty (contact), RuntimeTypes.ts is generated for those entities
Edit Phase 4: genpage-edit-planner agent is invoked via Task tool
Edit Phase 4: edit-planner reads page.tsx, config.json, and prompt.txt from the <page-id>/ folder
Edit Phase 4: genpage-edit-plan.md is written to the working directory root (not inside <page-id>/)
Edit Phase 5: Orchestrator reads genpage-edit-plan.md, genpage-rules-reference.md, RuntimeTypes.ts, and page.tsx
Edit Phase 5: Orchestrator applies edits inline using Edit tool on <working-dir>/<page-id>/page.tsx
Edit Phase 5: Search implementation uses Fluent UI V9 SearchBox or Input component
Edit Phase 5: Sort implementation uses column header click handlers or Fluent UI sorting
Edit Phase 5: Existing functionality is preserved per the edit plan's Preservation Constraints
Edit Phase 6: pac model genpage upload uses --page-id flag and --code-file points to <working-dir>/<page-id>/page.tsx
Edit Phase 6: Upload command omits --add-to-sitemap (existing page update)
Edit Phase 7: Browser verification is offered
Edit Phase 8: workflow-log.md is written

evals.prompt: Build a multi-step wizard form for creating new Case records. Step 1: customer info. Step 2: case details (title, priority, category). Step 3: review and submit. Use the incident and contact tables.
evals.expected_output: The orchestrator creates a working directory, invokes genpage-planner which validates prerequisites, authenticates, detects languages, gathers remaining requirements, detects that incident and contact entities exist, checks apps, presents a plan for a 3-step wizard, and writes genpage-plan.md. The orchestrator generates RuntimeTypes.ts for incident and contact entities, then invokes genpage-page-builder which reads the plan, schema, genpage-rules-reference.md and the wizard sample (2-wizard-multi-step.tsx), and generates a complete .tsx with step navigation (Next/Back), form validation, Fluent UI V9 form components, and dataApi.createRow for submission. Deploys with --data-sources 'incident,contact'.
expectations: A workflow-log.md file is saved documenting all phases attempted
Phase 0: Working directory created
Phase 1 (Planner): Prerequisites validated with separate commands
Phase 1 (Planner): Authentication confirmed, pac model list-languages run
Phase 1 (Planner): Question 2 is skipped (user provided description)
Phase 1 (Planner): Question 3 is skipped (user specified incident and contact tables)
Phase 1 (Planner): Plan includes 3-step wizard structure, form fields per step, Fluent UI form components
Phase 1 (Planner): genpage-plan.md is written to the working directory
Phase 2: Entity builder is SKIPPED because incident and contact entities already exist
Phase 4: pac model genpage generate-types --data-sources 'incident,contact' is run
Phase 4: RuntimeTypes.ts is read and column names are verified for both incident and contact entities
Phase 5 (Page Builder): genpage-rules-reference.md is read
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Wizard sample (2-wizard-multi-step.tsx or 3-poa-revocation-wizard.tsx) is read
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Generated code includes step state management (current step tracking)
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Next and Back buttons navigate between steps
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Step 3 (review) shows a summary of entered data before submission
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Submit uses dataApi.createRow with verified column names from RuntimeTypes.ts
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Form uses Fluent UI V9 components (Input, Dropdown, etc.) — not HTML form elements
Phase 5 (Page Builder): All form fields have aria-labels or aria-labelledby for accessibility
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Semantic HTML is used (button, section, etc.)
Phase 5 (Page Builder): async dataApi calls are wrapped in try-catch with error handling
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Generated code is a single .tsx file with export default GeneratedComponent
Phase 6: Upload includes --data-sources 'incident,contact'

evals.prompt: /genpage Build a task management board with columns for To Do, In Progress, and Done. Use the task entity. Allow dragging tasks between columns.
evals.expected_output: The orchestrator creates a working directory, invokes genpage-planner which validates prerequisites, authenticates, detects languages, gathers remaining requirements, detects the task entity exists, presents a plan for a kanban-style board, and writes genpage-plan.md. The orchestrator generates RuntimeTypes.ts for the task entity, then invokes genpage-page-builder which reads the plan, schema, and rules, and generates a complete .tsx with column-based layout, task cards, drag-and-drop using native HTML5 drag events (no external DnD libraries), and dataApi.updateRow to persist status changes.
expectations: A workflow-log.md file is saved documenting all phases attempted
Phase 0: Working directory created
Phase 1 (Planner): Prerequisites validated
Phase 1 (Planner): Authentication confirmed, pac model list-languages run
Phase 1 (Planner): Description is acknowledged from the command — question 2 is skipped
Phase 1 (Planner): Question 3 is skipped (user specified task entity)
Phase 1 (Planner): Plan describes kanban board with 3 columns mapped to task status values
Phase 1 (Planner): genpage-plan.md is written to the working directory
Phase 4: pac model genpage generate-types --data-sources 'task' is run
Phase 4: RuntimeTypes.ts is read — status field enum values are identified for column mapping
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Drag-and-drop uses native HTML5 events (onDragStart, onDragOver, onDrop) — no external DnD library
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Task cards show relevant fields verified from RuntimeTypes.ts
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Status update on drop uses dataApi.updateRow with the correct status enum value
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Code uses entity logical name 'task' (singular lowercase) not 'tasks'
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Responsive layout uses flexbox with relative units
Phase 5 (Page Builder): No 100vh or 100vw used
Phase 5 (Page Builder): All components and utilities are separate top-level functions (no nested functions)
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Generated code is a single .tsx file with export default GeneratedComponent

evals.prompt: Create a simple analytics dashboard with mock data. I just want some charts showing website traffic trends and visitor demographics. Keep it clean and minimal.
evals.expected_output: The orchestrator creates a working directory, invokes genpage-planner which validates prerequisites, authenticates, detects languages, confirms mock data usage, presents a minimal plan, and writes genpage-plan.md. Schema generation and entity builder are skipped. The orchestrator invokes genpage-page-builder which reads the plan and rules, and generates a clean .tsx with D3.js charts for traffic trends (line/area chart) and demographics (pie/bar chart), realistic mock data, and minimal Fluent UI styling. Deploys without --data-sources.
expectations: A workflow-log.md file is saved documenting all phases attempted
Phase 0: Working directory created
Phase 1 (Planner): node --version and pac help are run separately
Phase 1 (Planner): pac auth list is run and environment is confirmed
Phase 1 (Planner): pac model list-languages is run after confirming the environment
Phase 1 (Planner): Question 1 (new or edit) is asked
Phase 1 (Planner): Question 2 is skipped because user provided a description
Phase 1 (Planner): Mock data usage is confirmed (no Dataverse entities)
Phase 1 (Planner): Plan reflects minimal/clean design preference
Phase 1 (Planner): genpage-plan.md is written to the working directory
Phase 2: Entity builder is SKIPPED (mock data)
Phase 4: RuntimeTypes generation is SKIPPED (mock data)
Phase 5 (Page Builder): genpage-rules-reference.md is read
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Charts use D3.js exclusively — no Chart.js, Recharts, or other chart libraries
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Mock data is realistic (not empty arrays or identical values)
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Generated code has no unnecessary complexity matching the 'clean and minimal' request
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Code uses makeStyles with Fluent UI tokens for styling
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Generated code is a single .tsx file with export default GeneratedComponent
Phase 5 (Page Builder): No placeholders, TODOs, or ellipses in the generated code
Phase 6: Upload omits --data-sources flag

evals.prompt: Build a page showing all job candidates with their application status, interview scores, and assigned recruiter. I need new tables for this — cr_candidate and cr_jobrequisition entities don't exist yet in my environment.
evals.expected_output: The orchestrator creates a working directory, invokes genpage-planner which validates prerequisites, authenticates, gathers requirements, runs pac model list-tables --search to detect that cr_candidate and cr_jobrequisition entities do NOT exist, checks available apps, presents a plan showing entities to create (with columns, relationships, choices), and writes genpage-plan.md. The orchestrator invokes genpage-entity-builder which reads the plan, creates tables/columns/relationships in dependency order, asks about sample data, and returns. The orchestrator generates RuntimeTypes.ts, invokes genpage-page-builder to generate the .tsx, and deploys.
expectations: A workflow-log.md file is saved documenting all phases attempted
Phase 0: Working directory created
Phase 1 (Planner): Prerequisites validated
Phase 1 (Planner): Authentication confirmed
Phase 1 (Planner): pac model list-tables --search is run to check entity existence
Phase 1 (Planner): cr_candidate and cr_jobrequisition are identified as NOT existing
Phase 1 (Planner): pac model list is run to check available apps
Phase 1 (Planner): Plan includes entities to create with column definitions, types, and relationships
Phase 1 (Planner): Plan specifies a lookup relationship from cr_candidate to cr_jobrequisition
Phase 1 (Planner): genpage-plan.md is written to the working directory
Phase 2 (Entity Builder): genpage-plan.md is read to extract entity specifications
Phase 2 (Entity Builder): Dataverse plugin readiness is verified (.env, scripts/auth.py)
Phase 2 (Entity Builder): Independent table (cr_jobrequisition) is created BEFORE the dependent table (cr_candidate with lookup)
Phase 2 (Entity Builder): Propagation delay (3-5s) is observed after table creation before adding columns
Phase 2 (Entity Builder): Relationships are created after both tables exist
Phase 2 (Entity Builder): User is asked about sample data via AskUserQuestion
Phase 2 (Entity Builder): Actual logical names are reported back (may be normalized by Dataverse)
Phase 4: pac model genpage generate-types is run with the created entity names
Phase 4: RuntimeTypes.ts is read to verify correct column names
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Generated code uses ONLY verified column names from RuntimeTypes.ts
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Generated code is a single .tsx file with export default GeneratedComponent
Phase 6: pac model genpage upload includes --data-sources with the created entities

evals.prompt: Create a dashboard page with account metrics. I don't have any model-driven apps yet.
evals.expected_output: The orchestrator creates a working directory, invokes genpage-planner which validates prerequisites, authenticates, gathers requirements (account metrics with Dataverse), detects the account entity exists, runs pac model list which returns zero apps, asks the user whether to create a new app or cancel. User chooses to create. The planner includes 'create new app' in the plan and writes genpage-plan.md. The orchestrator creates the app via pac model create, generates RuntimeTypes.ts, invokes genpage-page-builder, and deploys to the newly created app.
expectations: A workflow-log.md file is saved documenting all phases attempted
Phase 0: Working directory created
Phase 1 (Planner): Prerequisites validated
Phase 1 (Planner): pac model list returns zero apps
Phase 1 (Planner): User is asked via AskUserQuestion whether to create a new app or cancel
Phase 1 (Planner): Plan indicates a new app needs to be created
Phase 1 (Planner): genpage-plan.md is written with app decision set to 'create new'
Phase 3: pac model create is run to create a new model-driven app
Phase 3: The new app-id is stored for deployment
Phase 4: pac model genpage generate-types --data-sources 'account' is run
Phase 5 (Page Builder): .tsx file is generated
Phase 6: pac model genpage upload uses the newly created app-id

evals.prompt: Build a project tracker page. I need new cr_project and cr_milestone entities with sample data to test the page.
evals.expected_output: The orchestrator invokes genpage-planner which detects cr_project and cr_milestone don't exist via pac model list-tables --search, presents a plan with entity specifications. The orchestrator invokes genpage-entity-builder which creates the entities, asks about sample data (user says yes since they explicitly requested it), and inserts realistic sample records. The orchestrator generates RuntimeTypes, invokes page-builder, and deploys.
expectations: A workflow-log.md file is saved documenting all phases attempted
Phase 1 (Planner): pac model list-tables --search confirms cr_project and cr_milestone do NOT exist
Phase 1 (Planner): Plan includes entity definitions for cr_project and cr_milestone
Phase 1 (Planner): Plan includes a relationship (cr_milestone has lookup to cr_project)
Phase 2 (Entity Builder): cr_project is created first (independent table)
Phase 2 (Entity Builder): cr_milestone is created second (depends on cr_project for lookup)
Phase 2 (Entity Builder): User is asked about sample data via AskUserQuestion
Phase 2 (Entity Builder): Sample data is created because user explicitly requested it
Phase 2 (Entity Builder): Sample records are realistic (not lorem ipsum or empty values)
Phase 2 (Entity Builder): Sample records respect relationships (milestones reference valid project IDs)
Phase 4: RuntimeTypes.ts is generated for both entities
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Generated code uses verified column names from RuntimeTypes.ts
Phase 6: Deployment includes --data-sources with both entity names

evals.prompt: Build a page for my new cr_widget entity that doesn't exist yet.
evals.expected_output: The orchestrator invokes genpage-planner which runs pac model list-tables --search to check if cr_widget exists. It does not. The planner records entity creation in the plan. In Phase 2a the orchestrator probes for Dataverse MCP availability. If the MCP tool is unavailable (plugin not installed), the orchestrator tells the user to install microsoft/Dataverse-skills and halts. If the MCP is available but returns an auth error (plugin installed but not connected), the orchestrator tells the user to run /dv-connect and halts. Only when the probe succeeds does the orchestrator invoke genpage-entity-builder.
expectations: A workflow-log.md file is saved documenting all phases attempted
Phase 1 (Planner): pac model list-tables --search 'cr_widget' is run
Phase 1 (Planner): cr_widget is identified as NOT existing (exact logical-name match, not fuzzy)
Phase 1 (Planner): Plan records cr_widget under Entity Creation Required
Phase 2a: Orchestrator probes Dataverse MCP list_tables tool availability BEFORE invoking entity-builder
Phase 2a: If Dataverse MCP is NOT installed, user is told to install microsoft/Dataverse-skills and workflow halts
Phase 2a: If Dataverse MCP is installed but returns auth error, user is told to run /dv-connect and workflow halts
Phase 2b: genpage-entity-builder is only invoked after the probe succeeds

evals.prompt: Build me three pages for a recruitment app: a candidate list page, an interview schedule page, and a hiring metrics dashboard. Use the contact and appointment entities.
evals.expected_output: The orchestrator creates a working directory, invokes genpage-planner which validates prerequisites, authenticates, detects that contact and appointment entities exist, checks available apps, presents a plan with 3 pages (candidate list, interview schedule, hiring metrics), and writes genpage-plan.md. The orchestrator generates RuntimeTypes.ts once for both entities, then invokes 3 genpage-page-builder agents in parallel (all in a single message). Each builder writes its own .tsx file. The orchestrator deploys all 3 pages sequentially.
expectations: A workflow-log.md file is saved documenting all phases attempted
Phase 0: A single working directory is created for all pages
Phase 1 (Planner): Plan includes 3 pages in the pages table
Phase 1 (Planner): Each page has a distinct file name, purpose, and entity assignments
Phase 1 (Planner): genpage-plan.md contains per-page specifications for all 3 pages
Phase 2: Entity builder is SKIPPED (contact and appointment entities already exist)
Phase 4: pac model genpage generate-types is run ONCE with --data-sources 'contact,appointment'
Phase 4: A single RuntimeTypes.ts is generated covering both entities
Phase 5: Three genpage-page-builder agents are invoked in a SINGLE message (parallel execution)
Phase 5: Each builder reads the same genpage-plan.md but extracts only its own page specification
Phase 5: Each builder reads the same RuntimeTypes.ts for column verification
Phase 5: Each builder writes a separate .tsx file to the working directory
Phase 5: No builder modifies another builder's file
Phase 5 (Page Builder): All 3 .tsx files use export default GeneratedComponent
Phase 5 (Page Builder): All 3 .tsx files follow genpage-rules-reference.md rules
Phase 6: All 3 pages are deployed via separate pac model genpage upload commands
Phase 6: Each upload uses the correct --code-file for that page
Phase 8: Summary table lists all 3 pages with their files, entities, and deployment status

evals.prompt: Build a page showing Account records with a filter toolbar and sortable columns.
evals.expected_output: This eval tests the plan revision loop. The orchestrator invokes genpage-planner. After presenting the initial plan via plan mode, the user requests a change (e.g., 'add a search box too'). The planner revises the plan and re-enters plan mode. The user approves the revised plan. The workflow then proceeds normally through Phases 2-8.
expectations: Phase 1 (Planner): Initial plan is presented via EnterPlanMode
Phase 1 (Planner): User requests a revision (simulated by returning changes-requested from ExitPlanMode)
Phase 1 (Planner): Planner revises the plan based on user feedback and re-enters plan mode
Phase 1 (Planner): Revised plan reflects the user's requested changes
Phase 1 (Planner): User approves the revised plan on the second presentation
Phase 1 (Planner): genpage-plan.md reflects the approved (revised) version, not the initial version
Phase 5 (Page Builder): Generated code implements the revised plan
